texto: Now that I have a 16-month-old son, my weekend ritual has changed - but it's better than ever. We get up early and go for a walk on one of the hiking trails near my home in Los Angeles, then meet up with friends at a diner. There's nothing better than sipping coffee, eating scrambled eggs, and taking three hours to do it.
explicacao: Citação de Connie Britton sobre temas ligados a amizade. “Now that I have a 16-month-old son, my weekend ritual has changed - but it's better than ever. We…” articula amizade, friends, son em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e termino.

frase_original: Now that I have a 16-month-old son, my weekend ritual has changed - but it's better than ever. We get up early and go for a walk on one of the hiking trails near my home in Los Angeles, then meet up with friends at a diner. There's nothing better than sipping coffee, eating scrambled eggs, and taking three hours to do it.

texto: For my birthday this year, my girlfriends - who knew I'd just inherited my dad's turntable - gave me a carton of albums like 'Blue Kentucky Girl,' by Emmylou Harris, and 'Off the Wall,' by Michael Jackson. It's all stuff we grew up with. I mean, you can't have a music collection without Prince's 'Purple Rain' - it just can't be done!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Connie Britton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amizade. O trecho “For my birthday this year, my girlfriends - who knew I'd just inherited my dad's turntable - gave…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, birthday, rain pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de aniversario e dia-chuvoso.
